description_long: This object is a pair of metal pliers with a robust, utilitarian design, commonly used for gripping, twisting, or turning objects. The handles are straight and flat, lacking any visible ergonomic or insulated features, and appear to be made entirely of metal. The jaws are wide and flat, with a smooth gripping surface and no serrations or wire-cutting notches, making them suitable for general gripping tasks rather than precision work. The pivot mechanism is a single, prominent bolt that allows the jaws to open and close, providing a simple but effective movement. The surface of the pliers is metallic and slightly matte, with some visible texture from the manufacturing process. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the tool.
description: A sturdy, all-metal pair of pliers with flat handles and wide, smooth jaws, featuring a single pivot bolt and a matte metallic finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the flat, straight handles and the wide, smooth jaws with the pivot bolt visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open, flat jaws and the symmetrical design of the gripping surfaces.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the flat handles and the jaw profile.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the jaws and the handle alignment, with the pivot bolt visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, flat metal handles with no insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Wide, flat jaws with smooth gripping surfaces and no serrations or wire-cutting features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed pivot bolt allowing basic opening and closing of the jaws.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with minimal visible texture.
